title: palicourea divisorana
intro: Palicourea divisorana, also known as the "divisor plant", is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as palicourea. The health benefits of Palicourea divisorana include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, wounds, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an antispasmodic and analgesic. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often administered to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
